results.title: Manager, Data Engineer at Arch Capital Group Ltd. - United States Remote
highlights:
Manager, Data Engineer at Arch Capital Group Ltd. - United States Remote
[...]
# Manager, Data Engineer
[...]
🏖️ New Jersey, New York, +2 more states – Remote🏖️ New Jersey – Remote 🗽 New York – Remote 🌲 North Carolina – Remote 🔔 Pennsylvania – Remote
[...]
### 📋 Description
[...]
• Lead delivery of high-quality data solutions by partnering with stakeholders and coaching data engineers. • Own end-to-end data engineering delivery across the project lifecycle. • Build strong partnerships across the organization to align priorities and deliver data-related goals. • Design clear, analytics-ready data structures by anticipating downstream analytical needs. • Evaluate and adopt new technologies and data sources to improve capability and efficiency. • Automate data ingestion and integration to reliably connect internal and external data sources. • Document data sources, definitions, and technical solutions to support transparency and reuse. • Reinforce strong delivery hygiene (version control, code review, automated testing, CI/CD, and operational readiness/monitoring). • Apply agentic, AI-assisted coding practices to accelerate delivery while maintaining appropriate controls. • Build agent-ready data assets, including semantic layer components (ontology, taxonomy, domain models) and governed access. • Provide retrieval-ready context (RAG pipelines, vector stores, knowledge bases) when needed.
[...]
### 🎯 Requirements
[...]
• 6+ years’ experience of hands-on development in Python and distributed processing environments (e.g., Spark) • 2–3+ years of technical leadership or project delivery ownership experience • Hands-on Databricks experience highly preferred • Strong programming expertise in Python and SQL, including data engineering frameworks, large-scale data manipulation, and governed AI-assisted development practices • Apache Spark proficiency (PySpark preferred) and experience with distributed data processing, including building scalable pipelines and optimizing performance for large-scale datasets • Cloud data platform proficiency (Snowflake, Databricks, Azure ecosystem fundamentals) • Data warehousing and modeling fundamentals (schema design, conformed definitions, performance optimization) • Data quality and observability practices (testing, reconciliation, monitoring) • Analytics & AI Readiness • Insurance data modeling for analytics and actuarial-ready data structures • MLOps familiarity supporting operationalized analytics and models • Semantic modeling skills (business definitions, metrics, ontology/taxonomy/domain models) • Business definition standardization for reuse across BI and AI use cases.

# Director, Strategic Analytics (Data Science)

Remote, USA

Posted 2026-05-27

Full-time

This a Full Remote job, the offer is available from: Connecticut (USA), New Jersey (USA), New York (USA), North Carolina (USA)

With a company culture rooted in collaboration, expertise and innovation, we aim to promote progress and inspire our clients, employees, investors and communities to achieve their greatest potential. Our work is the catalyst that helps others achieve their goals. In short, We Enable Possibility℠.

As Director, Strategic Analytics, you will play a crucial role in developing the team’s analytics capabilities. You will work on high-proﬁle advanced analytics projects that drive the company’s decision-making and strategy. You will work with or direct other data scientists to build best-in-class predictive models with creativity, statistical rigor and business sense. As a key member of the Strategic Analytics team, you will help advance our analytics capabilities and shape how Arch thinks about data and analytics.

highlights:
Enterprise Data Architect; Hybrid OR Remote- Jersey , Raleigh, or Hartford | Hirevector

# Enterprise Data Architect; Hybrid OR Remote- Jersey , Raleigh, or Hartford

Posted May 30, 2026

### Position: Enterprise Data Architect (Hybrid OR Remote- Jersey City, Raleigh, or Hartford)

- Enterprise Data Architect (Hybrid OR Remote
- Jersey City, Raleigh, or Hartford)

Arch Capital Group Ltd. invites applications for the Enterprise Data Architect role. This position is part of our Data Management team and will lead the architecture function to help Arch become a data‑driven, AI‑enabled organization.

- Location
- :
- Hybrid office or fully remote, must live near one of our North East offices (Raleigh, Jersey City, or Hartford).

- Base Pay Range
- : $122,000 - $149,000 per year. Additional compensation opportunities exist and are determined by location, scope, and qualifications.

- Location‑Specific Pay Bands
- North Carolina: $122,000 - $149,000
- Morristown, NJ: $142,000 - $173,000
- Jersey City, NJ;
- White Plains, NY: $157,000 - $180,000

- Team benefits include comprehensive medical, dental, vision and prescription coverage; a 401(k) plan with matching; paid parental leave, tuition reimbursement, voluntary time off, and more. Learn more about benefits.
- Duties & Responsibilities
- Collaborate with the Chief Data Architect to develop and execute the enterprise data management roadmap in alignment with Arch’s Data & AI strategy.
- Manage market‑solution architecture designs and playbooks for modern data warehousing technologies like Snowflake and associated analytics capabilities.
- Co‑ordinate delivery of data management initiatives with solution architects and program data architects.
- Own the target‑state enterprise data management platform, overseeing transition from current states and incorporating new capabilities as needed.
- Lead the Data Management Centre of Excellence, covering all data management and tooling aspects.
- Maintain vendor relationships across all data‑management tooling.
- Quantify and communicate the value of data platform transformations, shaping roadmaps in partnership with domain SMEs.
- Skills & Experience
- Strong data architecture expertise with a history of large‑scale transformative programming.
- Proven experience in Data Governance, Master Data Management, Data Quality, Metadata Management, Data Access Management, and Data Privacy for global enterprises.
- Deep technical knowledge of modern cloud data warehousing and ecosystem (Snowflake, Databricks, Azure services, dbt Cloud, Collibra).
- Hands‑on experience with data ingestion, transformation, visualization, and metadata management tools, preferably Snowflake.
- Experience building data products aligned to business requirements and the data mesh paradigm.
- Track record of leading successful technical adoption of modern data management capabilities.
- Senior stakeholder communication skills with executive reporting experience.

- To apply, please visit Arch Capital Group’s careers page or create a job alert for “Enterprise Data Architect” roles. Arch is an Equal Opportunity Employer and welcomes applicants from all backgrounds.
- #J-18808-Ljbffr

results.title: 2026-7923 Catastrophe Modeling Analyst-Mid (Nightshift) | Hirequorum
highlights:
2026-7923 Catastrophe Modeling Analyst-Mid (Nightshift) | Hirequorum

Hirequorum.← All Jobs

# 2026-7923 Catastrophe Modeling Analyst-Mid (Nightshift)

Posted Jun 7, 2026

### Company Description:

AGSI was incorporated in April 2016. We are committed to supporting the goals of Arch divisions through exceptional service delivery. We pride ourselves on maintaining flexibility and responsiveness to adapt to business unit and industry demands while focusing on sound project management. We are dedicated to growing and developing our employees as we build strong teams with strategic leadership.

### Job Description:

The Catastrophe Modeling Analyst quantifies catastrophe risk on Arch Re’s inward reinsurance contracts, analyzing client exposure and model outputs in third‑party platforms, researching catastrophe risk, and producing clear exposure reports. In parallel, the role leverages scalable data‑science and machine‑learning (e.g., similarity scoring, clustering) to clean, align, and analyze large exposure datasets, producing decision‑ready insights while fulfilling core catastrophe modeling duties

### Responsibilities:

- Leverage third-party catastrophe models to obtain estimates of catastrophe risk of Arch Re's cedents.
- Justify and explain the CAT model outputs and ensure that they are reasonable and correct.
- Generate other reports to give a complete picture of the cedents' catastrophe exposure.
- Handle with minimal supervision the cat pricing of reinsurance submissions.
- Apply machine‑learning methods (e.g., similarity scoring and clustering) to clean, align, and analyze large exposure datasets—producing reliable, decision‑ready insights.
- Produce concise analytics artifacts—written reports/summaries, interactive dashboards (e.g., Power BI, etc.), and reproducible notebooks (Python/R).

### Qualifications:

- Demonstrated analytical proficiency: intermediate Excel and practical experience with Python and/or SQL (R optional) for data preparation, analysis, and automation.
- Excellent attention to detail and organizational skills.
- Strong interpersonal and communication skills.
- Ability to produce clear, concise written reports/summaries and dashboards (e.g., Power BI, etc.) for non‑technical stakeholders.
- Interest in learning about reinsurance and catastrophe modeling.
- Ready to work outside of normal office hours during peak season, including evenings, weekends, and public holidays as necessary.
- Basic knowledge of insurance and/or reinsurance terminology.
- Flexible with changing requirements and ability to learn new processes quickly.
- Experience working with large, complex datasets and applying ML techniques to support data quality, alignment, and analytic insight generation is expected.
- Familiarity with data visualization tools (e.g., Power BI, etc.) is nice‑to‑have; exposure to data engineering (ETL, pipelines, warehousing) and process/report automation is beneficial.

### Additional Information:

- Graduate of any quantitative course, preferably in Mathematics, Actuarial Science, Computer Science, Information Technology, Physical Sciences, or Engineering.

Apply for This Job

results.title: 2026-7928 Data Engineer-Mid (Night Shift) | Hirevector
highlights:
2026-7928 Data Engineer-Mid (Night Shift) | Hirevector

Hirevector.← All Jobs

# 2026-7928 Data Engineer-Mid (Night Shift)

Posted May 29, 2026

### Company Description:

AGSI was incorporated in April 2016. We are committed to supporting the goals of Arch divisions through exceptional service delivery. We pride ourselves on maintaining flexibility and responsiveness to adapt to business unit and industry demands while focusing on sound project management. We are dedicated to growing and developing our employees as we build strong teams with strategic leadership.

### Job Description:

Have you ever wondered how insurance companies calculate the price of insurance policies?

The Corporate Actuarial Data & Technology Team is a dynamic, growing team at Arch. This team implements innovative insurance pricing models and analytical tools to improve profitability and growth across a wide range of Property & Casualty insurance products.

The Data Engineer will implement complex databases and data pipelines that put analytics at the heart of real-time decision-making. They will work closely with leaders across the company on high-profile analytics projects that drive business strategies. They provide technical guidance and ensure the use of sound engineering practices and effective use of resources. As a key member of the Corporate Actuarial Data & Technology data engineering team, they will advance our implementation capabilities and help our team deliver solutions faster.

### Responsibilities:

- Work with strategic partners to solve business problems by developing best-in-class data solutions
- Perform data manipulation using SQL and Python to develop data assets
- Leverage technology to automate data ingestion, transformation and integration
- Provide the appropriate level of documentation of sources and technical solutions
- Evaluate data quality and completeness
- Build strong partnerships with peers across the organization to support data-related goals
- Explore new technologies and data sources with curiosity and creativity
- Identify, design, and implement internal process improvements: automating manual processes and optimizing data pipelines
- Discover and explore new sources of data with curiosity and creativity
- Create and maintain optimal data pipeline architecture

### Qualifications:

- The ideal candidate has 1-2+ years of experience to 3-4+ years of experience of data/software engineering experience working with SQL, Python, and relational databases.
- Understanding and experience with dimensional data modeling (star schema)
- Familiarity with the MLOps framework
- Strong data manipulation skills for analytics
- Excellent critical thinking skills to tackle complex data challenges
- Strong organizational skills and very detail oriented
- Exceptional collaboration and relationship building skills
- Comfortable working in a fast paced, highly collaborative environment
- Ability to effectively communicate to different target audiences
- Resilient problem solving and critical thinking skills
- Flexibility - able to meet changing requirements and priorities

### Additional Information:

- BS in Computer Science, Information Technology degree, Data Analytics, Management Information Systems or equivalent

Apply for This Job

# Vice President, Head of Model Research & Event Response
[...]
Arch is seeking a Vice President, Head of Model Research & Event Response to lead the development, governance, and application of Arch’s catastrophe view of risk across regions, perils, and lines of business. This role is responsible for catastrophe model evaluation, change management, climate risk interpretation, and global event response, ensuring Arch’s risk insights are scientifically robust, business‑relevant, and decision‑ready.
[...]
As Arch continues to lean into AI and advanced analytics, this role will play a critical part in shaping how emerging technologies enhance model understanding, event response, and risk decisioning.

Engage with external research

, including CERCat participants, to stay current

- Set strategic priorities and ensure alignment with broader Catastrophe Analytics and enterprise risk objectives.
- Partner with Technology and Data teams to advance AI‑enabled analytics, automation, and next‑generation catastrophe tooling.
- Contribute to long‑term strategy around model innovation, climate analytics, and advanced risk insights.
[...]
- Bachelor’s degree in a quantitative field (e.g., Mathematics, Engineering, Atmospheric Science, Data Science, Actuarial Science, or similar).
- 10+ years of experience in catastrophe modeling, risk analytics, or related insurance/reinsurance roles.
- Deep expertise across multiple catastrophe modeling platforms (e.g., AIR, RMS, KCC, Oasis).
- Demonstrated experience leading event response and loss estimation for major catastrophe events.
- Strong leadership experience managing senior analysts or small expert teams.

- Experience supporting Lloyd’s or international insurance markets.

- Experience collaborating with academic or research consortia.

highlights: Lead within a Microsoft SQL Server environment in the Accident and Health Insurance business unit offers an exciting opportunity to drive data-driven decision-making, leverage insights, and contribute to the organization's strategic goals using Microsoft SQL Server, SSIS, SSAS, and Power BI. Responsibilities and Accountabilities: Ensure the quality of all data during its entire lifecycle. Facilitate month-end financial reporting. Facilitate integration with corporate Data Store. Support the business unit data warehouse and reporting including ETL processes, report design, development and testing. Create ad hoc reports and queries as needed. Assist with software development and data migration initiatives. Collaborate with business stakeholders, data scientists, analysts, and other technology teams to understand business requirements and translate them into effective data and analytics solutions. Assist Underwriting, Actuary, Sales, Claims, Customer Service and Marketing stakeholders in reporting and analytics initiatives Serve as a subject matter expert on business unit data when integrating with corporate data systems Design and optimize data integration, ingestion, and transformation processes using Microsoft SQL Server Integration Services (SSIS). Stay abreast of emerging trends, technologies, and best practices in data and analytics, specifically within the Microsoft SQL Server ecosystem, and proactively recommend innovative solutions to drive business value. Required Skills and Abilities: Strong expertise in data and analytics strategies, including data architecture, data modeling, and data integration within the Microsoft SQL Server environment. Proficiency in Microsoft SQL Server, including database design, SQL development, and performance optimization. Extensive experience with Microsoft SQL Server Integration Services (SSIS) for data integration and transformation. Familiarity with data visualization and reporting tools, particularly Power BI, within the Microsoft SQL Server environment. Strong understanding of data governance, data quality, and data security principles and best practices within the Microsoft SQL Server environment. Experience with cloud platforms and services (e.g., Azure, AWS) and their integration with Microsoft SQL Server is a plus. Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills, with the ability to effectively collaborate with both technical and business stakeholders. Knowledge of Power BI for interactive report development. Education and Experience: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, Engineering, or a related field. Advanced degree preferred. #LI-AM3 #LI-Hybrid For individuals assigned or hired to work in the location(s) indicated below, the base salary range is provided. Range is as of the time of posting. Position is incentive eligible. For Hunt Valley: $111,100 - $149,970 base + target bonus. Total individual compensation (base salary, short & long-term incentives) offered will take into account a number of factors including but not limited to geographic location, scope & responsibilities of the role, qualifications, talent availability & specialization as well as business needs. The above pay range may be modified in the future. Arch is committed to helping employees succeed through our comprehensive benefits package that includes multiple medical plans plus dental, vision and prescription drug coverage; a competitive 401k with generous matching; PTO beginning at 20 days per year; up to 12 paid company holidays per year plus 2 paid days of Volunteer Time Offer; basic Life and AD&D Insurance as well as Short and Long-Term Disability; Paid Parental Leave of up to 10 weeks; Student Loan Assistance and Tuition Reimbursement, Backup Child and Elder Care; and more. Click here to learn more on available benefits. Do you like solving complex business problems, working with talented colleagues and have an innovative mindset? Arch may be a great fit for you.

results.title: Risk Analyst job with Arch Capital Group | 5531225
highlights:
Risk Analyst job with Arch Capital Group | 5531225

You will need to login before you can apply for a job.

# Risk Analyst

Employer Arch Capital Group Location London, London, United Kingdom Salary Competitive Closing date 27 Jun 2026

View more categoriesView less categories

Save jobClick to add the job to your shortlist

## Job Details

With a company culture rooted in collaboration, expertise and innovation, we aim to promote progress and inspire our clients, employees, investors and communities to achieve their greatest potential. Our work is the catalyst that helps others achieve their goals. In short, We Enable Possibility℠.The main purpose of the role is to support the Risk Function's business risk partners to ensure the operation of an effective and robust enterprise risk management framework across Arch's Lloyd's, London Market and UK P&C businesses.Key tasks and responsibilities:

- Support in the ongoing development of the entity risk registers.
- Assist with the review of risk policies.
- Support the business risk partners in the quarterly risk assessments, risk reviews, controls assessments, KRI updates, and other risk management activities as required.
- Provide support to ensure that the ORSA process is undertaken in accordance with the ORSA Policy, including collation of information and preparation of the ORSA reports.
- Assist in the investigation of risk events, control failings or weaknesses, and monitoring risk function actions.
- Support the emerging risks process.
- Assist in preparing risk and control information for internal and external audiences including relevant Committees and the Board.
- Provide risk management training, and ongoing support, to stakeholders.
- Play a key role in the development and maintenance of the Risk Function's GRC tool (Decision Focus), including training system users.

Skills / Competencies:

- Knowledge of the components of a Risk Management Framework would be preferable.
- GRC tool experience, preferably Decision Focus.
- Strong written and spoken communication skills.
- Ability to prioritise and challenge.
- Must be aligned with Arch's values and behaviours, including being a committed team player and having a positive mindset.

Qualifications

- Preferably educated to degree level.

Experience

- At least one year of Risk Management experience in the insurance sector, preferably in Lloyd's or the London Market.

The ideal candidate will partner across product, data science, engineering, and business teams to translate strategic priorities into robust technical blueprints, with deep expertise in Databricks, AWS, Snowflake, and modern AI/ML platforms.

We’re excited to get to know you and connect your unique skills with our global opportunities. To create a modern and seamless experience, we use artificial intelligence (AI) in parts of our preliminary screening process. This technology helps us personalize job recommendations, automate interview scheduling, evaluate candidates based solely on experience—without considering name, gender, or other personal details—and provide real-time answers through our chatbot. AI is used only during early screening and never makes hiring decisions. Your RGA recruiter will work closely with you every step of the way to ensure the process feels personal, thoughtful, and focused on you.

results.title: Agentic and Generative Data Scientist II | Wfhverse
highlights:
Generative Data Scientist II

Remote, USA

Posted May 31, 2026

Full-time

 Description Position Summary Details We’re hiring a Agentic and Generative Data Scientist II to design and deploy AI systems end-to-end: from data preparation and evaluation to model fine-tuning, inference, and agentic workflows. You’ll partner closely with product and engineering to ship reliable, cost-effective, and scalable LLM-powered solutions on AWS. Requirements Essential Duties & Responsibilities The essential functions include, but are not limited to the following: End-to-end GenAI solutions: Scope problems, choose the right approach (prompt engineering, fine-tuning, agents), implement, evaluate, and deploy. Data & SQL: Write efficient SQL for analytics and data preparation; manage schemas and pipelines for model training and inference. Model training & fine-tuning: Run supervised fine-tuning (PEFT/LoRA/QLoRA), optimize prompts, and manage experiment tracking and evaluation. Agentic systems: Build agent workflows with tool use, memory, and safety/guardrails. Inference & deployment: Package services with Docker, optimize latency/cost (batching, caching, quantization), and deploy on AWS (ECS, EKS, SageMaker, Lambda with GPU acceleration). MLOps & Observability: Set up CI/CD for models/prompts, maintain offline/online evaluation pipelines, monitoring, and rollback strategies. Security & compliance: Implement data governance, PHI/PII protections, and guardrails against prompt injection and unsafe outputs. Cross-functional work: Collaborate with product managers and engineers to align GenAI capabilities with product goals; document clearly and communicate trade-offs. Production readiness: Lead conversations around scaling, monitoring, and maintaining GenAI systems in real-world environments. Minimum Qualifications (Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities) Education: Bachelor’s Degree or equivalent experience required; Master’s degree preferred. Experience: 5+ years of Software/ML engineering experience, including 2+ years building and deploying GenAI/LLM systems. MS/PHD in Computer Science or equivalent experience. Strong SQL and Python skills with solid software engineering fundamentals. Experience with agent frameworks (LangGraph, AutoGen, CrewAI) and building tool-driven agents. Hands-on with deep learning (PyTorch or TensorFlow) and LLM fine-tuning(SFT/PEFT like LoRA/QLoRA). Production experience with Docker and deploying on AWS (ECS, EKS,SageMaker, Lambda, or GPU services). Experience creating Data and Model pipelines for model training anddeployment at scale. Familiarity with prompt engineering, evaluation frameworks (LLM-as-judge,metrics), and offline test harnesses. Understanding of security & compliance for sensitive data (e.g., PHI/PII) and safe deployment of AI systems. Excellent problem-solving, communication, and documentation skills. Inference optimization: quantization (bitsandbytes, GPTQ/AWQ), batching, caching, or vLLM. Healthcare experience: familiarity with HIPAA, medical data handling, or working in health tech. Experiment tracking (MLflow, W&B), CI/CD for ML, and monitoring (Prometheus, Grafana). Familiarity with major LLM APIs and OSS models (OpenAI, Anthropic, Llama, Mistral). Other: Tech Stack Languages: Python, SQL DL/LLM: PyTorch, Tensorflow, Hugging Face, PEFT/TRL, vLLM Data: Snowflake, Postgres Cloud: AWS (ECS, EKS, SageMaker, Lambda) MLOps: Docker, CI/CD, MLflow or W&B Supervisory Responsibilities This role does not have any direct reports and is a single contributor role. Working Environment and Travel Requirements Work is typically in a normal office administrative environment involving minimal exposure to physical risks. Position requires little to moderate physical activity. Mostly sedentary work exerting up to 10 pounds of force occasionally or a negligible amount of force to lift, carry, push, pull, or otherwise move objects. Work involves sitting most of the time, but may involve walking or standing for brief

# Senior Data Scientist/AI Engineer - Generative AI
[...]
ARNVIND GROUP

Senior Data Scientist/AI Engineer - Generative AIEXO ARNVIND is collaborating with a pioneering financial technology leader in Sweden to identify their next Senior Data Scientist/AI Engineer specialising in Generative AI. Our client is shaping the future of AI-driven solutions in the financial sector, where cutting-edge technology meets real-world impact. This is a rare chance to join a high-caliber team, work on transformative generative AI projects, and influence how AI is deployed at scale in a regulated, high-stakes environment. If you are passionate about building production-grade AI systems and want to work where innovation meets execution, this role is for you.The OpportunityAs a Senior Data Scientist/AI Engineer focused on Generative AI, you will play a pivotal role in designing, developing, and deploying AI applications that redefine how AI is leveraged in financial services. You will collaborate with a talented, multidisciplinary team to create robust, scalable, and compliant AI solutions. This position offers the opportunity to work on high-impact projects, mentor colleagues, and establish best practices in generative AI-all while operating in a dynamic, forward-thinking organisation.Key Responsibilities
[...]
- Design, build, deploy, and monitor production-ready AI applications that deliver measurable business value.
[...]
- Develop and implement evaluation frameworks to ensure the quality, reliability, and performance of AI models.
[...]
- Create and refine tooling, architecture, and best practices for generative AI, setting the standard for the team and organisation.
[...]
- Document AI models, processes, and workflows to meet regulatory and compliance requirements.
[...]
- Identify and prototype innovative generative AI use cases, turning ideas into tangible solutions.
[...]
- Provide guidance, support, and mentorship to teammates, fostering a culture of collaboration and continuous learning.
[...]
What You Bring
[...]
- A Master's degree or PhD in a relevant field such as Computer Science, Data Science, Machine Learning, or Artificial Intelligence.
- Proven expertise in generative AI, large language models (LLMs), and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG).
- Strong proficiency in Python and hands-on experience with deep learning frameworks and LLM APIs.
[...]
- Experience designing and implementing agentic systems, structured testing, and model evaluation methodologies.
[...]
- A solid background in machine learning, experimentation, and systems design.
[...]
- Ability to work in a regulated environment, with a focus on compliance, risk management, and documentation.
[...]
- Experience with cloud platforms (e.g., AWS, Azure, Google Cloud) and distributed systems.
- Familiarity with MLOps practices and tools for model deployment and monitoring.
- Prior work in the financial services or fintech sector, particularly in areas requiring compliance and risk awareness.
[...]
- Impact: Work on high-visibility projects that shape the future of AI in financial services, with the opportunity to see your work deployed at scale.
[...]
- Growth: Access to career development opportunities, including mentorship, training, and the chance to lead initiatives in generative AI.
[...]
- Team: Collaborate with a diverse, high-performing team of AI engineers, data scientists, and technologists who are passionate about innovation.
[...]
- Tech Stack: Work with cutting-edge tools and technologies, including generative AI, LLMs, RAG, and agentic systems, in a modern, cloud-enabled environment.
[...]
LocationThis role is based in Stockholm, Sweden, with a hybrid working arrangement that allows for flexibility between remote and office work.Ready to Make an Impact?If this opportunity excites you, apply now and let us take it from here.
